en:
Platform Positioning (What it is)
↓
IP Tour (How — Core Strategy)
├── Dual-track IP collabs (global IP into China + Chinese IP going global)
├── Chinese IP commercial proof (Douluo Dalu + Scissor Seven)
├── Virtual concerts + brand partnerships
└── Creator economy ecosystem
↓
Budget Deployment (How resources are allocated)
├── Overview: $30M USD + four-pillar strategic priority
├── Paid media (50% — core user acquisition)
├── Esports (15% — foundation ecosystem)
├── Content UGC (15% — ecosystem building)
└── IP launch/tour (20% — cold start)
↓
All-Ages Ecosystem (compliance flip)
↓
Technical Alignment (feasibility assurance)
(Table of contents chapter list — see source file "Contents" section; retain clickable structure if outputting Markdown)

"Egg Party" (50M creators, 100M UGC maps) and "Peacekeeper Elite Oasis Origin" (33M DAU editor ecosystem) have cultivated tens of millions of UGC creators and established a monetizable commercial loop
This proves Chinese players have deeply accepted the logic of "creating content inside games" — no need to explain "why build" from scratch
China's market environment is transformed — player openness to innovative gameplay, UGC ecosystem maturity, and cultural IP global influence have all reached new heights.

As a Chinese company combining platform, R&D, and publishing, our values align closely with Epic's; we have China-rooted, globally-facing game publishing capability, and can activate China's quality player base through a nimble, open partnership model.
We are ready to explore new possibilities for Fortnite's China publishing with Epic.
Sausage Man has operated for 8 years in cartoon-rendered BR (peak MAU 27M), proving Chinese players' long-term payment willingness and community loyalty for non-realistic shooters
In 2024, VALORANT achieved 50M MAU with "hero shooter + trendy style", further validating that stylized shooting has crossed the "market education" stage and become a mainstream option
Based on the **"pragmatic narrative"** principle (no inflated expectations, controlled messaging, emphasize "eating the cake well" over "everyone's blockbuster"), reframe market analysis:

When Fortnite first reached China in 2018, the market had just emerged from PUBG's realism aesthetic; cartoon rendering + building mechanics created a double unfamiliarity that raised the barrier to entry. Today, this premise has fundamentally changed:

"Heartopia" (80M+ China downloads, 5M international first-week adds) validates the scalability of free-form building systems in China, proving building works as standalone content (not just a competitive tool)
**Non-realistic shooter + long-tail UGC platform = X.D.'s exclusive domain**

In China, the only publisher with both "cartoon shooter long-term live ops experience" + "zero-rev-share platform ecosystem" is XD Inc.. These are the two necessary conditions for Fortnite's success in China, and X.D. is the only team that satisfies both.
**Proposal theme:** "China's First Cross-Media Interactive Platform" — together we build the Fortnite platform for China
XD Inc. × Epic Games | Fortnite China Publishing Proposal
Peacekeeper Elite · Oasis Origin editor promo
Peacekeeper Elite and Delta Force raised the quality floor. Sausage Man, Egg Party, and Oasis Origin proved stylized shooters + UGC/creator appetite.

Style-diverse. Educated audience.
Non-realistic & UGC at scale.
When Fortnite first reached China, benchmarks for non-realistic shooting, UGC, and creator platforms were thin. Reference: PUBG Mobile (realism-first).

Realism-led shooter market.
Cartoon + build = high barrier.
Proof that stylized shooters sustain — aligns with Fortnite's cartoon-rendered, mode-platform logic.
Nine-figure creator and map supply validates UGC mindset; Oasis Origin editor ecosystem reinforces "party + creator" loop.
Building and social at scale accepted by young users — Zero Build and social loops map directly.

Shared Values · TapTap Scale ·
Why XD Inc.
Anti-platform-tax · long-term products · UEFN creator payout
No channel tax on dev earnings · healthier LTV/CAC
Sausage Man collab & brand partnership showcase
Collab depth preview (86+ case pipeline reusable)

Sausage Man:
China's Closest Parallel to Fortnite
Sausage Man · Steam page hero visual
Ultraman · Kamen Rider · Sanrio · Douluo Dalu · Assassin Wulinxiao
China-scale validation · international launch · #1 iOS free in 50+ countries (US, JP, KR included)
China-scale validation: free-form building × casual social hit; building works as standalone content, not just competitive accessory.
International launch + first-week growth: simultaneous global ship, multi-market execution.
#1 iOS free in 50+ countries (US, JP, KR): cross-border user acquisition and publishing reach — prioritizing global breadth over single-market depth.
UE4 · 13 seasons live · China / EU / KR simultaneous launch — shares UE tech stack and seasonal rhythm with Fortnite
UE4 seasonal live ops + CN/US/KR simultaneous launch; China stays in sync with global version and release cadence.
Post-IP-acquisition commitment: seasonal content, pipeline throughput, and cross-border delivery — not a single-region sprint.
Shares UE tech stack and long-season planning with Fortnite; engineering and ops methods transfer directly.
Global season-based live ops × simultaneous multi-region launch — make China part of the global ecosystem, not a "special edition".
86+ collab cases · full chain from licensing to live → global IP into China + Chinese IP going global
Cross-region legal, asset, and derivative coordination; long-term live ops cadence supports Fortnite global IP entry into China.
Ragnarok Online × Disney collab Key Visual
China collab execution and review-scale templates ready for "Chinese IP going global" delivery.
Sausage Man × Scissor Seven collab promo
FMCG × gaming — single-case KV (vertical 2:3, aligned with left two columns).

notes: Brand name 'Shuanghui' is 9 chars vs 8 limit; alternative: 'Shuangh' (incomplete) — recommend keeping 'Shuanghui'

en: **Opportunity**: Don't fight for the saturated tactical-combat **red ocean** — use the game as a base, content interaction as core, creators and cross-border collabs as growth engines to open a **new perception category** and long-term platform ecosystem.
**Audience**: Mature appetite for fused online entertainment; users stay and pay not just to win, but for social, expression, participation, spectatorship, and cultural identity.

**Positioning**: The optimal solution is not a pure competitive wrapper — it's a **next-gen interactive platform** that sustains content events, carries a creator ecosystem, and connects brand partnerships.
Game, Creator, Social, Culture — four images horizontal
Year-one budget: hover sector or legend to highlight

We recommend a first-year budget of $30 million USD, allocated as a "launch burst + long-tail ecosystem" dual-engine strategy. The budget is not pure paid acquisition — it's a systematic layout around Fortnite's platform positioning. (Merge: keep or map to per design)
Build broad market awareness, drive pre-reg and download conversion, support major release pushes.
Create break-out events, IP tie-ups, offline experiences, and launch social buzz.
Rapidly build China-local tournament infrastructure; deepen core player affinity and long-term vitality.
Creator content supply, livestream/video distribution, and UEFN creator ecosystem.
Launch Strategy ($6M): Zero Build + IP / Offline
**Tier-1 domestic IP launch collabs**: Local anime, film, and TV IPs enter Fortnite — manufactured buzz + monetization hooks.
**Offline "shareable events"**: Pop-ups, immersive installations, limited-themed venues — extend online heat into participatory, check-in-worthy experiences.
**Content supply & distribution**: Reach broader circles; feed short-video, livestream, and social platforms with sustained material to amplify launch-window noise.
Visuals: Zero Build launch, offline immersion, China anime IP ref
**Zero Build as launch lead**: Aligns with China's mature shooter market; lowers build-learning barrier; widens new-user entry.
**Layered content rollout**: High-intensity fast-paced combat first; build mode, creative maps, and full UGC ecosystem follow in stages.
**Avoid day-one complexity overload**: Use a clear progression path to retain depth players rather than dumping everything at once.
UEFN creation tools and map development
Visuals: UEFN full · livestream + TapTap dual
Streaming & video incentive · audience and approach
**Audience**: Livestream hosts, short-video creators, guide and entertainment content creators.
**Goal**: Rapidly build multi-layer content matrix; amplify reach and topic diffusion.
**Challenge**: SAC-style programs are relatively unfamiliar to Chinese users — awareness and participation threshold are high.
**Solution**: Combo of content incentives + SAC to gradually establish SAC among Chinese creators.
Mode creator incentive · audience targets and approach
**Audience**: Deeper ecosystem participants — local mode/map creators and professional developers (including TapTap dev resources).
**Goal**: Build China-local creator supply; upgrade from "an officially published game" to "a sustainably growing mode platform".
**Challenge**: Not just short-term exposure — need to establish UEFN creation habits and sustainable content + monetization paths.

**Solution**: Combine Creator Economy 2.0 and In-Island Transactions; use UEFN to build shareable modes around local culture and hot topics; partner with TapTap to recruit Chinese game developers into Fortnite content creation.

Open to all players; format, rewards, and ruleset mirror the international version. The goal is to let every player feel and participate in Fortnite's unique competitive atmosphere — and gain something from it.
Independent China pro competitive cycle representing Fortnite China's highest tier; serves as the entry point to global FNCS. The platform for Chinese pros to compete on the world stage.

Mid-size tournaments co-hosted with livestream platforms, top streamers, and content hubs — balancing spectacle and shareability. The goal is to raise Fortnite's core-circle discussion heat through content-platform events.

Tournaments serve more than elite players — they help Fortnite China build a "playable, watchable, followable" competitive narrative. This deepens core user stickiness and continuously supplies high-quality content for livestream, video, and social platforms.
50% of the first-year budget goes to paid media, but spend is not averaged — it's phased around product lifecycle and key nodes to ensure every dollar serves stage-specific objectives.

Target casual gamers, returning legacy players, and content-platform audiences via brand videos, pre-registration campaigns, KOL seeding, and core media exposure — establish the first wave of "Fortnite is back in China" awareness.
Maintain market presence; cover festival nodes; support community activity; sustain reach to high-potential segments.
Ensure year-round heat continuity; preserve growth foundation for next major release and Year 2.
Convert awareness rapidly into downloads, activations, and server-opening buzz. Amplified by streamer livestreams, short-video spread, and launch celebration events for maximum market ignition.

Re-awaken dormant users and re-amplify content potential. Re-distribute around new release selling points, new activities, new collabs, and community highlight moments — avoid the "launch-day peak only" single-curve trap.
Easy Anti-Cheat + THEMIS (XD in-house security platform)
Transferred from Sausage Man's 8-year live environment
Standards aligned with China regulation on political / sexual / copyright content
Seasons and major updates: same cadence as global; individual collabs scheduled separately per licensor timelines.
Hotfixes: trunk-aligned; payment, real-name, security patches via China-specific channel.
Multiplayer: China-local by default; cross-border only where policy permits.
UEFN: create → review → publish pipeline; China content pool connects to global Discover via agreed rules.
Content flow: machine pre-check + human review; two-way circulation boundaries fixed upfront.
Anti-cheat & logs: EAC + THEMIS (in-house security platform); login via WeChat/mobile/minor-protection — Epic account narrative stays intact.
Global where possible; local where needed.

China Delivery: Global Cadence Sync ×
UEFN Content Pipeline
Fortnite Battle Pass UI — selectable reward tracks
Seasons and patches follow global trunk; content and accounts use compliant pipes and agreed stacks.
